items.title: Search intensifies for missing hiker as operation enters critical phase
items.summary: TAPAH: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while climbing Gunung Batu Putih on May 23, has entered a critical phase after she was lost for more than 72 hours. Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said the SAR operation had been intensified, with additional personnel deployed and the search area expanded to locations where the victim may seek food and water. She said the search would now focus on areas along Sungai Ayer Busok, where the victim may have gone in search of water.

items.title: Government drafting initiatives to help media practitioners navigate AI, economic challenges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government, through the Communications Ministry, is formulating initiatives to help media practitioners navigate current challenges, including economic uncertainty and the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said this year's National Journalists' Day (Hawana) comes amid concerns among media practitioners over their jobs and future in the industry. Fahmi said the existence of a free and ethical media forms the cornerstone of the country's democratic system.

items.title: The great migration: Why Singapore giants like Gardenia are moving to Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 29 — In Senai’s sprawling industrial belt, Gardenia Malaysia’s massive bakery sits alongside warehouses operated by tech giant Seagate and e-commerce heavyweight Shopee. Last week, the bread maker announced it would shift production from its Pandan Loop facility in Singapore to Johor Bahru, a move resulting in 141 retrenchments in the republic. “Moving operations to Johor makes strong commercial sense,” said Tan, CEO of Olive Tree Property Consultants.

items.title: AI giant Anthropic reaches near-trillion dollar valuation
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic said Thursday it had raised US$65 billion in a new funding round that values the Claude maker at US$965 billion, more than its archrival OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT. Founded by former OpenAI employees, including chief executive officer Amodei, Anthropic has a special focus on AI safety even as it rushes out new products to remain in the AI race. Anthropic's near-trillion-dollar valuation puts it ahead of OpenAI, which was valued at US$852 billion in March.

items.title: Unrecognised Alexandria University pharmacy programme leaves Malaysian students in limbo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Pharmacy Board (PBM) was not consulted over the Higher Education Ministry's offering of the clinical pharmacy (PharmD) programme at Alexandria University in Egypt. The board's Pharmacy Services Programme Division said this in response to concerns that the university's PharmD programme is not recognised in Malaysia. "At present, Alexandria University offers new pharmacy programmes, namely the Bachelor of Pharmacy - PharmD and Bachelor of Pharmacy - PharmD (Clinical Pharmacy).

items.title: US imposes fresh sanctions on Iran's military oil sales, Treasury says
items.summary: The Treasury Department said it had sanctioned eight vessels involved in transporting Iranian crude oil and petroleum products to global markets. The vessels included the Marshall Islands-flagged oil tanker Flora, the Comoros-flagged crude oil tanker Hauncayo and the Panama-flagged tanker Ill Gap. The Treasury Department said some of the sanctioned Iranian entities also use the oil sales infrastructure of the Iranian armed forces to secure oil products from outside Iran.

items.title: Pahang Sultan extends condolences, aid to surviving children of couple killed in Pekan crash
items.summary: The Pahang Sultanate, in a Facebook post, said the contribution from His Royal Highness was presented by the Sultan of Pahang’s special private secretary, Tok Aria Rakna Ahmad Norbadelin Abdul Murad, to the family of Inspector Syah Lokman Ludza Ramli, 50, and his wife Nurulfiza Ardiana Ambak, 46, at the Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) here yesterday. According to the post, two of the couple’s children, Mohammad Adib Syah, 18, and Mohammad Aqif Syah, 10, are currently receiving treatment at the hospital for injuries sustained in the incident. Syah Lokman Ludza, who was attached to the Kuantan District Police Headquarters, and Nurulfiza Ardiana were reported to have died at the scene due to severe injuries.

items.title: Elderly woman missing for three days found safe in oil palm estate
items.summary: PONTIAN: Elderly woman Nathiah Selamat, 77, who went missing for three days in Kampung Seri Bunian here, was found safe at 10.50am yesterday. Villager Mohamed Suib Kamso, 50, who works at an oil palm plantation in Parit Wakaf, Kampung Tenggayon, said he noticed an unusual sound nearby. "She started walking through the oil palm plantation in Kampung Medan Sari, then Kampung Seri Bunian, before finally being found in the oil palm plantation area in Kampung Parit Wakaf, Tenggayon," he said.

items.title: Melaka police: Girl, 14, injures herself after adoptive mother bars meeting with boyfriend
items.summary: MELAKA, May 29 — A 14-year-old girl, believed to be under the influence of ‘mushroom’ vape, slashed her neck and wrists after being stopped by her adoptive mother from meeting her 17-year-old boyfriend at a house in Jonker Walk, Banda Hilir here. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said that in the 10.33 pm incident on Sunday (May 24), the victim, upon being prevented from meeting her boyfriend by her 49-year-old adoptive mother, went into a bedroom on the first floor of the house, before injuring herself with a knife. “The victim was taken to the Melaka Hospital and her condition is reported to be stable,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: KPMG Australia says CEO and audit head quit over whistleblower investigation
items.summary: SYDNEY: Accounting firm KPMG Australia said on Friday its chief executive officer and head of audit quit after an investigation into complaints raised by a whistleblower about sharing client data fell short of its standards, in a blow to the country's professional services sector. Chief executive officer Andrew Yates, who joined the firm in 1990 and became chief executive officer in 2021, resigned after its investigation into the whistleblower's allegations "fell short of the firm's expectations, those of the whistleblower and the broader community", the firm said in a statement. The firm's managing partner of audit and assurance, Julian McPherson, also stepped down and would leave the firm after an orderly transition, the statement added.

items.title: Man, 23, missing at sea off Langkawi after waves sweep him away while retrieving jetski
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 29 — A man is feared to have drowned after he tried to retrieve a jetski that drifted off in the waters near Langkawi today. Langkawi Fire and Rescue Department Zone 4 chief Fire Supt Mohd Zamri Abdul Ghani said they received a call about the incident at 9 pm and a team of five firefighters from the Persiaran Putra Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the location about 22 minutes later. “The water rescue team will begin search and rescue operations tomorrow morning to locate the victim,” he added.

items.title: Inflation pressures building in New Zealand even if Iran war ends soon
items.summary: WELLINGTON: Inflation pressures are building in New Zealand even if the Middle East conflict ends soon, a senior central banker said on Friday, adding that she would be looking at high-frequency data to see if a July rate hike would be warranted. Three external members voted for a rate hike. Silk said she saw inflation pressures building in the New Zealand economy due to higher oil prices, but there was some real uncertainty around the duration of the Middle East conflict.

items.title: APAD probes LRT derailment near Chan Sow Lin, demands full report from Rapid KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) has launched a thorough investigation into the Light Rail Transit (LRT) train derailment near the Chan Sow Lin station on Thursday morning, warning of strict enforcement action if negligence is found. APAD said it conducted a site inspection immediately after the incident, led by its director-general, alongside representatives from the Rail Enforcement and Rail Planning divisions, to assess the situation and ensure immediate remedial actions were taken by the operator. Should any violations be found, enforcement action may be taken under Section 101 of the Land Public Transport Act 2010.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against US dollar on improved sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the greenback on Friday amid news about the possible United States-Iran truce, which is expected to have a positive impact on the local note, said an analyst. At 8am, the local currency rose to 3.9755/9815 against the greenback from Thursday's close of 3.9770/9805. "News on the 60-day US-Iran truce has led to a decline in Brent crude oil prices by 1.04 per cent to US$93.31 per barrel.

items.title: Mitsui seeks LNG investments to meet power demand from data centres
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese trading house Mitsui is looking to invest in LNG projects across the Middle East, the US and Australia to meet rising power demand from data centres, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing its chief executive officer Kenichi Hori. The company will consider taking equity stakes or securing supply agreements in LNG and gas chemicals firms, Hori told Bloomberg News, adding that demand for LNG was booming as companies sought clean energy to power AI infrastructure. Mitsui already has an interest in Abu Dhabi National Oil Co's Ruwais liquefied natural gas (LNG) export facility and will consider further investment in the Middle East, Hori told Bloomberg News.

items.title: Costco gas stations draw more frugal Americans as prices bite
items.summary: NEW YORK: Costco Wholesale reaped the spoils of surging gas prices, as more consumers sought out its lower-priced fuel, helping it top market expectations for third-quarter US sales. The final five weeks of the quarter were Costco's highest ever in terms of sales volume, chief executive officer Ron Vachris said on an earnings conference call on Thursday. Gas prices exceeding US$4 per gallon, on the rise as the Iran war drags on, drove many Costco members to use its gas stations for the first time, Vachris said.

items.title: US, Iran near deal but Trump sign-off awaited
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States and Iran closed in on a framework for a ceasefire extension detail Thursday but President Donald Trump has yet to approve any agreement, US officials said. US sources told AFP earlier that the two sides had reached agreement on a memorandum of understanding (MOU) to prolong the truce for 60 days. But US Vice President JD Vance said Trump -- who was notably silent throughout the day -- had yet to sign on the dotted line.

items.title: Oil prices mixed amid conflicting US-Iran ceasefire deal reports
items.summary: NEW YORK: Oil prices settled mixed on Thursday after a choppy trading session, as traders mulled conflicting reports of progress on a potential deal to extend a ceasefire between the US and Iran. Brent crude futures for July, which expire on Friday's settlement, closed down 58 cents, or 0.6 per cent, at US$93.71 a barrel. US oil futures eked out marginal gains to settle up 22 cents, or 0.3 per cent, at US$88.90 a barrel.

items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq hit record highs on extended US-Iran ceasefire
items.summary: NEW YORK: The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq posted record closing highs on Thursday after news reports said the US and Iran had reached a draft agreement to extend their ceasefire for 60 days, while investors also digested key inflation data. The agreement still needs US President Donald Trump's approval, sources told Reuters. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 24.69 points, or 0.05 per cent, to 50,668.97, the S&P 500 gained 43.27 points, or 0.58 per cent, to 7,563.63 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 242.74 points, or 0.91 per cent, to 26,917.47.

items.title: US dollar weakens on reports of US-Iran ceasefire deal
items.summary: The euro gained 0.2 per cent against the dollar to US$1.1649. Against the Swiss franc, the dollar weakened 0.37 per cent to 0.784. US Treasury bond yields fell.

items.title: Leadership effectiveness without micromanaging staff
items.summary: Leadership is often misunderstood as the constant supervision of people and processes. However, excessive control can create frustration, reduce confidence, and weaken the very performance leaders are trying to improve. Effective leadership is not about controlling every decision.

items.title: Petronas Suriname Block 52 may lift Malaysia's energy role [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd's (Petronas) oil exploration activities at offshore Block 52 in Suriname could position Malaysia within a globally significant emerging energy corridor, strengthening its role in the international upstream oil and gas sector. Economist Samirul Ariff Othman told Business Times that developments at Suriname's offshore Block 52 are strategically important for Petronas for few reasons. Samirul said the block could play a significant role in Petronas' international upstream strategy, particularly in terms of geographic diversification.

items.title: FMM urges tax relief as supply chain disruptions hit manufacturers [WATCH]
items.summary: FMM president Jacob Lee Chor Kok told Business Times that the industry body has also requested a double tax deduction on extraordinary logistics costs, saying these unplanned expenses are weighing heavily on manufacturers' working capital amid ongoing supply chain disruptions. Lee said transport bottlenecks have intensified as haulage operators exhaust subsidised diesel allocations earlier than expected, driven by longer port waiting times and rerouted supply chains linked to the prolonged Middle East conflict. "Already, 70 per cent of manufacturers have reported severely deteriorating supply conditions.

items.title: Austrian jihadist jailed for 15 years for Taylor Swift concert attack plan
items.summary: WIENER NEUSTADT, Austria: An Austrian court on Thursday sentenced a 21-year-old who admitted plotting a foiled jihadist attack on a Taylor Swift concert to 15 years in jail. Accused of planning the attack and forming an IS cell, the jury found Austrian national Beran A. guilty on all counts after deliberating for hours. Beran A. went on trial last month on terror offences and other charges in Wiener Neustadt outside Vienna.

items.title: Trump's face could appear on US$250 bill
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump could soon appear on a new US$250 bill, in the Republican's latest move to shatter US traditions by putting his personal stamp on national institutions. A proposal for the new bill, featuring a glaring Trump, was first reported Thursday by the Washington Post. "Right now there is proposed legislation -- front of the House, in front of the Senate -- to change the first requirement so that a living person, Donald J. Trump, could be on a US$250 bill," US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told a media briefing Thursday.

items.title: Test kits key to sniffing out fentanyl [WATCH]
items.summary: Asian Criminological Society board member Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy said even a very small amount of fentanyl could suppress breathing and lead to death within minutes. He said unlike traditional narcotics, fentanyl could be easily mixed into other drugs, pills or even vape liquids without the users' knowledge, significantly increasing the risk of overdose. Sundramoorthy said synthetic drugs such as fentanyl were highly profitable products for organised crime groups.

items.title: From ‘bah’ to ‘kamek’: The many differences between Sabahans and Sarawakians (VIDEO)
items.summary: But for many in peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak are still casually bundled together as “East Malaysia” — or worse, reduced to the catch-all category of “others” on forms and official documents. “Malayans often see Sabah and Sarawak as one cultural block called ‘East Malaysia’, but to Sabahans and Sarawakians, the differences are obvious,” said Universiti Malaysia Sabah linguistics professor Jane Wong. “They share Borneo, but they do not share one single culture, one single language, or one single identity,” she said.

items.title: US Treasury chief threatens to sanction Oman if it joins Hormuz tolls
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Thursday threatened to target ally Oman if it helped impose a tolling system in the key Strait of Hormuz, warning of sanctions against all parties involved in such actions. Teheran's retaliatory actions have engulfed the region in violence and seen Iran virtually block the strait, sending global energy and fertilizer prices soaring. Thursday's threat by Bessent came hours after the Treasury sanctioned Iran's "Persian Gulf Strait Authority," Teheran's new agency that collects fees for transiting the strait.

items.title: EU sanctions seven Israeli settlers, organisations
items.summary: BRUSSELS, Belgium: The EU formally sanctioned seven Israeli settlers and organisations on Thursday over violence against Palestinians and settlement expansion in the Israeli-occupied West Bank. The sanctions consist of travel bans and asset freezes, and EU citizens and companies are also banned from making funds, financial assets or other economic resources available to those listed. The EU also sanctioned 10 representatives of the Palestinian militant group Hamas, the European Council, representing the 27 member states, said.

items.title: Penang expands list of heritage items to 50
items.summary: The gazettements on May 7 bring the number of items under the Penang State Heritage Enactment 2011 to 50, comprising 15 historical sites, seven intangible cultural heritage and 28 dishes. New landmarks added to the list are Penang Free School, Penang High Court, Kapitan Keling Mosque, Acheen Street Malay Mosque, Khoo Kongsi, the Penang State Museum Board building and St George's Church. "Although the Penang State Heritage Enactment 2011 was passed in 2011, no official state-level cultural heritage gazettement had been carried out under the enactment over the past 15 years.

items.title: NHRA seeks tenancy act
items.summary: The National House Rental Association (NHRA) has urged the government to expedite the enactment and implementation of a residential tenancy act (RTA) to protect homeowners from being penalised for illegal activities carried out by tenants. NHRA president Prakash P. Kalivanan highlighted growing public concern over residential properties being used for illicit operations, including Bitcoin mining, prostitution, harbouring undocumented immigrants, storing stolen goods and loan-sharking activities. He said under current legal provisions, property owners could face investigation or legal action if authorities determined they had knowledge of the unlawful activities or failed to take reasonable preventative steps.

items.title: TM accelerating AI and digital innovation capabilities
items.summary: Managing director and group chief executive officer Amar Huzaimi Md Deris said TM is entering a new growth phase centred on artificial intelligence (AI), digital innovation and sustainability. In 2025, TM distributed about RM2 billion in shareholder and economic value. In 2025, TM recorded a revenue growth of 11.4 per cent to RM11.9 billion, while pre-tax profit stood at RM2 billion and operating cash flow was at RM2.5 billion.

items.title: Australia charges woman over Islamic State links
items.summary: SYDNEY: Police yesterday charged a woman linked to the Islamic State (IS) group with membership of a terrorist organisation and entering a known conflict zone. A joint counter-terror police team said they would allege that the woman, 34, travelled to Syria between 2013 and 2014 with a man to join IS. The woman was detained by Kurdish forces in 2019 and held in the al-Hawl Internally Displaced Persons camp until returning to Australia last September, they said.

items.title: RON95 subsidy adjustment is last resort, says govt adviser
items.summary: Prime Minister's Office senior economic and finance adviser Nurhisham Hussein said Putrajaya currently has several policy options available but is proceeding cautiously given the sensitivity surrounding fuel prices and broader cost-of-living pressures. "About 90 per cent of consumers using fuel in Malaysia are not affected by the 200-litre RON95 quota," he said speaking to Khairy on the show. He said that any rationalisation involving petrol subsidies would likely be deferred for as long as possible.

items.title: Govt demands probe into alleged abuse of Malaysian volunteers by Israeli forces
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is demanding an immediate probe into the alleged harsh treatment of Malaysian volunteers detained by Israeli forces during a recent humanitarian mission to Gaza. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail says the government will not compromise on protecting the safety, welfare, and rights of Malaysians abroad, in line with international legal principles and universal humanitarian values. "The government also demands that the welfare, dignity, and legal rights of all detained activists be fully respected and protected," he said in a Facebook post yesterday.

items.title: Mafia boss' network assets worth over US$232mil seized in Italy
items.summary: ROME: Italian authorities have seized assets worth more than €200 million (US$232 million), allegedly linked to the Mafia empire and drug trafficking network of late Cosa Nostra boss Matteo Messina Denaro, police said on Thursday, reported German Press Agency (dpa). Three people were arrested during a large-scale operation carried out across several countries, authorities said. The recent raids were part of an investigation into money laundering linked to drug trafficking operations allegedly connected to Messina Denaro, authorities said.

items.title: Putrajaya demands investigation into Malaysians detained by Israel in Gaza flotilla mission, insists rights and dignity be protected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Madani Government remains firm and will not compromise on protecting the safety, welfare and rights of Malaysians abroad, in line with international legal principles and universal humanitarian values. Minister of Home Affairs Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the government demands an immediate, comprehensive, independent and transparent investigation into the harsh treatment of Malaysian citizens and Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists detained by Israeli forces during a recent humanitarian mission to Gaza. “The government also demands that the welfare, dignity and legal rights of all detained activists be fully respected and protected,” he said in a post on his Facebook page today.

items.title: Putrajaya and Selangor must act fast to ‘lock in’ proposed PJ public hospital, says Kampung Tunku rep
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kampung Tunku assemblyman Lim Yi Wei today urged Putrajaya and the Selangor government to move quickly on plans for a new public hospital in Petaling Jaya, warning that delays in land matters and funding could derail the project. Welcoming the recent announcement of a finalised site for the proposed hospital, Lim said the land should be immediately rezoned as a hospital site under the “Institutional and Public Facilities Zone”. Lim also warned that building a new hospital alone would not resolve deeper structural problems in the healthcare system.

items.title: One wild ‘Odyssey’: PJ police nab four suspected thieves after car chase from Kota Damansara to Air Panas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Four local men were arrested after driving dangerously and fleeing from a police patrol car from Kota Damansara to Air Panas here early this morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the incident began at 2.23am when members of a police patrol car tried to inspect a black Honda Odyssey multi-purpose vehicle (MPV). “The police’s swift action, however, succeeded in intercepting the vehicle at Jalan Air Jernih, Air Panas, Kuala Lumpur before arresting the four suspects,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Missing Gunung Batu Putih hiker may be searching for water after 72 hours, says Perak Bomba chief
items.summary: TAPAH, May 28 — The condition of female hiker, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while climbing Gunung Batu Putih here on May 23, has entered the critical phase after being lost for over 72 hours. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Datuk Sayani Saidon said that in the face of this, the search and rescue operation has been intensified, with more personnel deployed and the search area expanded to areas where the victim may look for food and water. The search will now focus on the area along Sungai Ayer Busok, where the victim may have gone to find water.

items.title: After hot day, MetMalaysia forecasts stormy night across central and southern peninsula, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Thunderstorms are forecast in parts of Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Negeri Sembilan, Johor, Sarawak and Sabah until 9pm, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said this afternoon. Affected areas in Perak include Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar. In Terengganu, the warning covers Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman.

items.title: Closure of 30 pig farms in Tanjong Sepat enters final phase, with 11,000 animals remaining
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT, May 28 — The closing down of pig farms in Tanjong Sepat, here is in the final phase with only 11,000 remaining animals involved and is expected to be fully completed soon. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Exco Datuk Izham Hashim said the closure involves about 30 farms that are still operating in various stages, after the eviction order was issued due to the remaining livestock not being mature enough to be disposed of since February. Earlier, Tanjong Sepat assemblyman Datuk Borhan Aman Shah was reported to have said that all pig farms in Tanjong Sepat would be completely closed by the end of June before the enforcement phase is implemented starting July 1.

items.title: LRT users call for better track maintenance after Chan Sow Lin derailment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Users of the Light Rail Transit (LRT) service on the Ampang/Sri Petaling line want maintenance and repair works on the track system to be improved after a train derailed near the Chan Sow Lin Station this morning. A Bernama observation also found that repair work was being carried out by Rapid KL at the incident location, about a three-minute drive from the Chan Sow Lin Station towards Cheras. It was also found that many users still use the train service, which now operates as a shuttle service between Pudu, Chan Sow Lin and Cheras stations.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in eight states, KL until 6pm today, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Eight states and Kuala Lumpur are expected to experience thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds until 6pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). MetMalaysia, in a warning issued at 3.10pm, stated that the weather conditions are forecast to hit Bandar Baharu in Kedah, while in Perak, it involves Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, and Hilir Perak. The same warning was also issued for Petaling in Selangor, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin, alongside Segamat in Johor.

items.title: No ‘business-as-usual’: Transport minister orders maximum action against Prasarana after LRT derailment near Chan Sow Lin (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today ordered the “maximum” punitive action against Prasarana Malaysia Bhd following the derailment of an LRT train near Chan Sow Lin station, warning that no member of the rail operator’s top management would be spared accountability over the incident. “I have also directed the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) to take the maximum punitive action against Prasarana Malaysia Bhd over this incident,” he said in a statement today. Prasarana Malaysia Bhd is the state-owned public transport operator that runs the Klang Valley’s LRT, MRT and Monorail rail networks, as well as Rapid KL bus services.

items.title: Rapid KL deploys extra staff, keeps free buses and alternative trains running as Ampang-Sri Petaling repair works continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL said alternative train services and free shuttle buses will remain active until the end of today following the derailment near Chan Sow Lin station that disrupted services on the Ampang–Sri Petaling Line earlier this morning. “Repair works are still actively ongoing,” Rapid KL said, adding that “alternative train services and shuttle buses remain active until the end of operating hours”. It also activated several shuttle train services between affected stations, including Bandar Tasik Selatan and Cheras, Ampang and Maluri, as well as Maluri and Chan Sow Lin.

items.title: GOF raids in Kelantan uncover drugs, undocumented migrants and RM1.1m timber haul
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade carried out four separate operations involving drugs, illegal immigrants, ketum juice and illegal logging activities in Kelantan, with total seizures amounting to RM1.93 million. Its commander, SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain, said the operations were conducted under “Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan” (OTWK) and “Op Bersepadu Khazanah” (OBK), involving cooperation with various enforcement agencies. Ahmad Radzi said in the second case, 19 Myanmar nationals, comprising 13 men and six women aged between 17 and 40, were detained near Kampung Jeram Perdah, Pasir Mas on May 20.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DKU says no special meeting on May 29 as matter now before courts
items.summary: In the letter dated May 26, DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin said the proposed special meeting requested by several Negeri Sembilan royal and customary leaders could not be convened due to ongoing court proceedings. “DKU is unable to convene a special meeting as requested in view of the ongoing court proceedings,” he wrote. Raja Norazli also said the individuals involved could not independently convene or call for a DKU meeting, adding that any purported special meeting scheduled for May 29 would not be recognised as an official DKU meeting.

items.title: Delivery man pleads not guilty to threatening salon assistant in Sentul
items.summary: A. Vinodvarma, 36, is accused of committing the act against Ashha Begum, 24, at Taman Bamboo, Off Jalan Ipoh, Sentul, at 6.20pm on May 22. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail to the accused, as the offence falls under the non-bailable category. However, Vinodvarma’s lawyer, Erickka Farrise Amir, sought bail, saying that her client is the family’s breadwinner and poses no flight risk.

items.title: Thirteen-year-old girl allegedly beaten by group of students after ‘misunderstanding’, say Kota Kinabalu police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kota Kinabalu police are investigating the alleged assault of a 13-year-old girl who was reportedly beaten by five female students in an incident that surfaced after a video went viral on social media. Police said they received a report from the victim at about 7.51am on May 23, a day after the incident allegedly took place in the city. Initial investigations found the girl was allegedly assaulted by five students from several different schools in the Kota Kinabalu area following what police described as a misunderstanding and lingering dissatisfaction among those involved.

items.title: Submissions for phase four of Bantuan Kasih Johor extended until June 28, says MB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 28 — The Johor state government has extended the period for the submission of Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) Fourth Phase until June 28 to give more time to recipients to obtain the assistance. “At least 35 review counters and 35 payment counters will be provided for locations with more than 3,000 recipients, while locations with 2,000 recipients and below will involve 10 review counters and 10 payment counters,” he said in a statement after chairing the Fourth Phase BKJ Coordination Meeting here. Onn Hafiz said the operating hours would also be extended to 12 hours, especially in the Johor Bahru district, to reduce congestion and provide comfort to recipients, especially the elderly.

items.title: Nine hurt, including suspected migrant smuggler, after GOF car chase ends in crash near Jeli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Nine men were injured, three of them seriously, after a car believed to be transporting undocumented migrants crashed near Kampung Sungai Rual in Jeli, Kelantan, this morning. Southeast Brigade General Operations Force commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the remaining victims suffered minor injuries and were treated at Jeli Hospital. He said a General Operations Force (GOF) team from the Seventh Battalion had spotted a suspicious Proton Saga on the East-West Highway.

items.title: ‘Does not tolerate disrespect’: FamilyMart Malaysia suspends employee over viral soup-throwing row with delivery rider
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — FamilyMart Malaysia has suspended an employee accused of throwing soup at a delivery rider following a dispute captured in a viral social media video, according to FMT. “Our team is investigating the incident with urgency and the staff (allegedly) involved has been placed off duty,” FamilyMart Malaysia said in a statement to the media organisation. The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online today showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.

items.title: In Sarawak, three men arrested for suspected trespass into house compound in Tudan Phase 3
items.summary: MIRI, May 28 — Police arrested three local men aged between 21 and 31 on Monday for suspected trespassing into the compound of a house in Tudan Phase 3 here. Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report on Sunday night after the homeowner encountered the suspects at his residence at around 9.30pm. Acting on information received, a team from the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Miri District Police Headquarters arrested the three men at Kampung Tudan here.

items.title: Malaysian, 22, charged with murder after woman found dead in Singapore HDB lift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A 22-year-old Malaysian man has been charged with murder after a 21-year-old woman was found dead at a Housing and Development Board (HDB) block in Singapore’s Choa Chu Kang estate earlier this week. According to The Straits Times (ST), Mohamad Faiz Umar was charged in court today over the death of Chua Bee Ting, who was allegedly killed inside a lift at Block 248 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 2 on the night of May 26. Preliminary police investigations indicated that Faiz had allegedly stabbed Chua.

items.title: DoE issues RM4.59m in fines after more than 5,000 inspections in first four months of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 28 — The Department of Environment (DOE) conducted more than 5,000 inspections nationwide and recorded accumulated fines amounting to RM4.59 million involving environmental pollution cases during the first four months of this year. “Based on the inspections and investigations conducted, the DOE has taken 3,149 follow-up enforcement actions against parties found to be non-compliant with the Environmental Quality Act 1974,” the statement said. In efforts to curb the illegal entry of scheduled waste into the country, DOE said 51 inspection operations were conducted at major ports involving inspections on 702 containers suspected of carrying scheduled waste.

items.title: June rollout of fentanyl test kits signals tougher stance on synthetic drugs, says Nada D-G
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) will begin using specialised testing kits nationwide next month to detect fentanyl and other emerging synthetic drugs, amid mounting concerns over substances being mixed into vape liquids. Ruslin said the kits were capable of detecting fentanyl, synthetic cannabinoids, cocaine and psilocybin — the psychoactive compound found in so-called “magic mushrooms”. “Previously, there were claims that Nada could not test for fentanyl and synthetic substances.

items.title: Man reportedly gored to death by runaway buffalo in Johor, attack video goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A man was reportedly gored to death by a runaway buffalo in an incident believed to have taken place in the Ulu Tiram area yesterday, with footage of the attack later circulating widely on social media. The victim was later seen getting back to his feet following the attack, although he is understood to have suffered serious injuries. Online , he later died from injuries sustained in the incident.

items.title: Immigration Department says AI-altered passport photos could lead to legal action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Immigration Department has urged the public not to use photographs generated or altered using artificial intelligence (AI) technology when applying for Malaysian passports. In a Facebook post, the department said the use of AI-generated images or the submission of false information in passport applications was a serious offence that could lead to action under the Passport Act 1966 (Act 150). “Any false photo or information submitted may result in the application being rejected, and the applicant may face action under existing legal provisions,” the department said.

items.title: Missing for three days, Pontian woman, 77, found safe in oil palm plantation
items.summary: Pontian Baru fire and rescue station senior fire officer Mohd Khairul Anwar Ab Ghani said the victim, identified as Nathiah Selamat, was found on the third day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation. The operation commander said the victim was found within a 2.4km radius east of the operation’s incident control post. Mohd Khairul Anwar said the SAR operation involved eight fire and rescue personnel, including the department’s K-9 unit.
